scope: Extending interaction features to generalized polynomial expansion. Discusses the trade-off between increased model expressiveness (capturing non-linear relationships) and the risk of feature explosion (curse of dimensionality).
key_concepts: Polynomial features are generated by taking existing features to specific powers and multiplying them with each other up to a certain degree.
They allow fundamentally linear models to learn non-linear decision boundaries by projecting the data into a higher-dimensional space.
The number of features grows combinatorially with the degree of the polynomial and the number of original features, leading to a risk of feature explosion.
Feature explosion increases memory consumption, training time, and the risk of overfitting.

definitions.definition: Features created by raising existing features to a power and generating interaction terms up to a specified degree.
The combinatorial growth in the number of features when applying transformations like polynomial expansion, which can degrade model performance.

examples.description: Given features A and B, a degree-2 polynomial expansion creates new features: A^2, B^2, and A*B.

relations.builds_toward: The discussion on the risk of feature explosion in ch06_s07 and dimensionality reduction in Chapter 8.

figures.caption: Pertumbuhan Jumlah Fitur pada Ekspansi Polinomial
figures.description: Grafik kurva eksponensial yang menunjukkan bagaimana jumlah fitur turunan meroket seiring peningkatan derajat polinomial pada dataset dengan 10, 50, dan 100 fitur mentah.

exclusions: Kernel trick or SVM kernels (keep the focus purely on explicit feature construction).
Detailed derivation of the combinatorial formula for feature counts.
